title: Family remembers woman killed in Mattapan carjacking crash as a ‘beautiful soul’
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News Newly available footage shows Mabinty Janneh trying to run from the carjacking suspect who allegedly struck and killed her in Mattapan Saturday.
Mabinty Janneh, 32, was killed in a crash in Mattapan Saturday.

title: Bill Simmons ‘not convinced’ Jaylen Brown stays with Celtics after Giannis trade drama
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics "I am not convinced he’s going to be on the team in October.
I think it’s 50/50.
” Jaylen Brown and Giannis Antetokounmpo have been linked in trade rumors for weeks.

title: Coca-Cola factory in Northampton finally shuts down, laying off 175 employees
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Business The shutdown ends decades of operations and marks a significant economic loss for the city.
Bottles of Coca-Cola.

Paul Yeung/Bloomberg Years after Coca-Cola announced plans to shut down its Northampton bottling plant, the facility will officially close by the end of the year, eliminating 175 jobs and dealing a blow to the city’s coffers and manufacturing sector.

title: No Scotland, no party: The Dubliner closes for a one-day break after Tartan Army takeover
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: World Cup “Thankfully there’s nothing happening in Boston today,” the downtown pub added cheekily ahead of the England-Ghana World Cup match.
A Scotland soccer fan, center in floral shirt, celebrates Croatia’s goal during a World Cup game against England at The Dubliner Irish pub in Boston on June 17, 2026.
Sophie Park / The New York Times By Abby Patkin June 23, 2026 | 3:51 PM 1 minute to read The Dubliner has officially spoken: No Scotland, no party.

title: Homelessness decreases in Boston, annual point-in-time census finds
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Boston officials reported a 19% decrease in overall homelessness since 2021.

Mayor Michelle Wu, Public Health Commissioner Bisola Ojikutu, and Assistant Director for Street Homelessness Initiatives Jim Greene speak to an unhoused man during the 2026 Homeless Census in January.
Andrew Burke-Stevenson for The Boston Globe ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Robert Kraft wants to bring Patriots to Scotland for a football game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots The NFL has nine international games planned for the 2026 season, with more on the way in the coming years.
Scotland fans took in two games in Foxborough during group stage action in the 2026 World Cup.

(Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff) By Conor Ryan June 23, 2026 | 3:00 PM 2 minutes to read The Tartan Army took over Boston for over a week — with an outpouring of bagpipe-blasting, kilt-wearing, traffic-cone-placing revelry standing as a highlight of the ongoing World Cup festivities here.

title: Massachusetts family loses four members in Dominican Republic fire
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The victims were staying at an Airbnb when a fire broke out in the early morning hours of June 13.
GoFundMe organizer Natasha Salas shared this vacation collage of family members as part of her effort to raise funds for the survivors of a fire in the Dominican Republic.

title: One injured in stabbing near downtown Nantucket ferry terminal
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Nantucket police have detained a person in connection with the incident.
Adobe Stock By Abby Patkin June 23, 2026 | 2:28 PM 1 minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.
One person was rushed to Nantucket Cottage Hospital Tuesday following a stabbing in the island’s busy downtown area, according to police.

title: Revolution president Brian Bilello on the Everett stadium, summer transfers, and leveraging the World Cup
